assertions.title: Token with no price returns 404 via fail_to_None

steps.expected: HTTP 404 with error message about no price found

steps.action: Check container logs for exception stack traces
steps.expected: No exception stack traces; only a warning-level 'price_not_found' log
steps.observed: Container logs show: 'failed to get price for [ZERO_ADDRESS]' (from ypricemagic) and structured warning 'price_not_found' with request_id. No exception stack traces.

steps.action: Verify None return is not retried (unit test: TestFetchPriceNoneReturn::test_none_return_no_retry)
steps.expected: get_price called exactly once (no retry for None return)
steps.observed: Unit test passes — mock.assert_called_once() confirms no retry for None return

evidence.containerLogs: warning: price_not_found, request_id=4fe14738-7716-488e-af5e-0a3ab5c0cba8, token=0x00000000, block=18000030. No exception stack traces.

assertions.title: Invalid price values still rejected

steps.action: Attempted to validate from API surface
steps.expected: GET /price returning 502 when get_price returns NaN/Inf/negative
steps.observed: Cannot trigger NaN/Inf/negative return from get_price via user-surface curl requests — these values come from internal ypricemagic computation

steps.action: Ran unit tests as alternative evidence (TestFetchPriceInvalidValues)
steps.expected: 4 tests pass: nan, inf, -inf, negative all raise ValueError
steps.observed: All 4 unit tests PASSED: test_nan_raises_value_error, test_inf_raises_value_error, test_negative_inf_raises_value_error, test_negative_raises_value_error

steps.action: Verified server.py endpoint handler catches ValueError and returns 502
steps.expected: Code path: ValueError with 'Invalid price value' or 'Negative price' → JSONResponse(status_code=502)
steps.observed: Confirmed in server.py lines: except (RetryError, ValueError) catches it, 'Invalid price value' in msg or 'Negative price' in msg → 502

evidence.unitTests: TestFetchPriceInvalidValues: 4/4 PASSED (nan, inf, -inf, negative all raise ValueError). TestFetchPriceNoRetryForValueError: 1/1 PASSED (ValueError not retried).
evidence.codeReview: server.py _fetch_price: math.isnan/isinf/negative checks → ValueError. /price endpoint: catches ValueError → 502 with error envelope.

assertions.issues: Cannot be proven from user-surface-only behavior. The assertion requires mocking get_price to return NaN/Inf/negative, which is impossible via curl. Unit tests (which pass) are the correct evidence type per the validation contract.

assertions.title: Retry logic preserved for transient errors

steps.action: Attempted to validate from API surface
steps.expected: Observe retry behavior on transient ConnectionError/TimeoutError/OSError
steps.observed: Cannot trigger transient RPC errors from user-surface curl requests — these are internal infrastructure failures

steps.action: Ran unit tests as alternative evidence (TestFetchPriceRetry)
steps.expected: 4 tests pass: ConnectionError retries+succeeds, TimeoutError retries+succeeds, OSError retries+succeeds, exhausted retries raises RetryError
steps.observed: All 4 unit tests PASSED: test_connection_error_retries_and_succeeds, test_timeout_error_retries, test_os_error_retries, test_connection_error_exhausts_retries

steps.action: Verified retry decorator configuration in server.py
steps.observed: Confirmed in server.py: @retry(stop=stop_after_attempt(2), wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=1, max=4), retry=retry_if_exception_type((ConnectionError, TimeoutError, OSError)))

steps.action: Verified None return does NOT trigger retry (unit test)

evidence.unitTests: TestFetchPriceRetry: 4/4 PASSED. TestFetchPriceNoneReturn: 2/2 PASSED (None not retried). TestFetchPriceNoRetryForValueError: 1/1 PASSED (ValueError not retried).
evidence.codeReview: server.py @retry decorator configured correctly: stop_after_attempt(2), retry_if_exception_type((ConnectionError, TimeoutError, OSError)). Only exceptions trigger retry — None return passes through immediately.

assertions.issues: Cannot be proven from user-surface-only behavior. The assertion requires mocking get_price to raise ConnectionError on first call then succeed, which is impossible via curl. Unit tests (which pass) are the correct evidence type per the validation contract.

assertions.title: New params are optional — existing requests unaffected

steps.expected: HTTP 200 with standard response shape: {chain, token, block, price, cached, block_timestamp}

steps.action: Verify all expected response keys present
steps.expected: Keys: chain, token, block, price, cached, block_timestamp
steps.observed: All 6 keys present. No missing keys, no extra unexpected keys.

steps.action: Verify response field types and values
steps.expected: price is numeric, block matches request, chain is ethereum
steps.observed: price=1.0 (float), block=18000030 (matches request), chain='ethereum' (correct), block_timestamp=1693067255 (valid Unix epoch)

evidence.responseValidation: All 6 expected keys present (chain, token, block, price, cached, block_timestamp). price is numeric, block matches request, chain is 'ethereum'. Response is superset of pre-upgrade shape with additive block_timestamp.

frictions.description: First unresolvable-token test (0x0000...0001) timed out after 120s — ypricemagic's exhaustive pricing attempts for some addresses exceed nginx's proxy_read_timeout of 120s. Had to use the zero address (0x0000...0000) which resolves faster.
frictions.resolution: Used 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 (zero address) instead, which fails quickly in ypricemagic

frictions.description: After the timed-out request, subsequent requests to the same container returned 502 'Chain backend is unavailable' from nginx — likely the container was still processing the hung request. Had to wait ~10s for recovery.
frictions.resolution: Waited for container to finish processing the previous request before retrying

blockers.description: VAL-CORE-005 and VAL-CORE-006 require mocking get_price internals (returning NaN/Inf/negative, or raising ConnectionError) which cannot be done through curl API requests. These assertions' evidence types are explicitly 'unit test with mocked get_price', making them inherently non-testable from the user surface. All relevant unit tests pass (9/9).
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Ran the existing unit tests (TestFetchPriceInvalidValues: 4 passed, TestFetchPriceRetry: 4 passed, TestFetchPriceNoRetryForValueError: 1 passed) as alternative evidence confirming the behaviors are correctly implemented.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-CORE-004: unresolvable token returns 404 with no exception traces; VAL-CORE-015: existing requests work identically with no new params), 2 blocked (VAL-CORE-005, VAL-CORE-006: require unit-test-level mocking of get_price internals, not achievable from API surface — all 9 relevant unit tests pass as alternative evidence).
